General guidelines.

   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.

      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo link-assistant/hive-mind --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an ./examples and/or ./experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to ./experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to ./examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

Initial research.
   - When you start, make sure you create detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step, make sure that as many points from these guidelines are added to your todo list to keep track of everything that can help you solve the issue with highest possible quality.
   - When user mentions CI failures or asks to investigate logs, consider adding these todos to track the investigation: (1) List recent CI runs with timestamps, (2) Download logs from failed runs to ci-logs/ directory, (3) Analyze error messages and identify root cause, (4) Implement fix, (5) Verify fix resolves the specific errors found in logs.
   - When you read issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. IMPORTANT: Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML). Use a CLI tool like 'file' command to check the actual file format. Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's \"Not Found\" pages saved as .png) can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and will crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download FAILED — do NOT call Read on this file. Instead: (1) For images from GitHub issues/PRs (URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\"), these require authentication — retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) If retry still fails, skip the image and note it was unavailable.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1478.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner link-assistant [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has THREE different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/pulls/1479/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1479/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/pulls/1479/reviews --paginate
      IMPORTANT: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" ONLY returns conversation comments and misses review comments!
   - When you need latest comments on issue, use gh api repos/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1478/comments --paginate.

   - When issue is solvable, implement code with tests.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.

      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.

   - When you finish, create a pull request from branch issue-1478-5fc2faa92a88. (Note: PR 1479 already exists, update it instead)

GitHub CLI command patterns.
   - IMPORTANT: Always use --paginate flag when fetching lists from GitHub API to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/\${owner}/\${repo}/pulls/\${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

                   await log(`   Verify attempt ${verifyAttempts}: Could not parse PR data, retrying...`, { verbose: true });\n  1244→                    }\n  1245→                  }\n  1246→ cause diagnosis\n  1255→                const verifyStderr = lastVerifyResult && lastVerifyResult.stderr ? lastVerifyResult.stderr.toString().trim() : '';\n  1256→                const stderrInfo = prCreateStderr ? ` (gh pr create stderr: ${prCreateStderr.trim()})` : '';\n  1257→                const verifyInfo = verifyStderr ? ` (gh pr view stderr: ${verifyStderr})` : '';\n  1258→                throw new Error(`PR verification failed - gh pr create returned URL \"${prUrl}\" but PR #${localPrNumber} does not exist on GitHub after ${maxVerifyAttempts} verification attempts${stderrInfo}${verifyInfo}`);\n  1259→              }\n  1260→              // Store PR info globally for error handlers\n  1261→              global.createdPR = { number: localPrNumber, url: prUrl };\n  1262→              await log(formatAligned('✅', 'PR created:', `#${localPrNumber}`));\n  1263→              await log(formatAligned('📍', 'PR URL:', prUrl));\n  1264→              if          await log('     • The GitHub CLI enforces strict assignee validation');\n  1275→                await log('');\n  1276→                await log('  🔧 How to fix:');\n  1277→ The key points regarding encoding are:\n\n1. **JSON Format**: The API uses JSON format for request bodies, and JSON text SHALL be encoded in Unicode, and the default encoding is UTF-8.\n\n2. **UTF-8 Support**: UTF-8 is maximally compatible with standard Git tools, and this applies to the GitHub API as well.\n\n3. **Unicode Handling**: For APIs accepting JSON data, a URI string must contain only a defined subset of ASCII characters, and any other characters must be replaced by an escape sequence representing the UTF-8 encoding of the character.\n\n## Practical Recommendations\n\nWhen creating a pull request with Cyrillic Russian characters using the GitHub API:\n\n- Use UTF-8 encoding for your JSON request body\n- Send the title and body fields with Russian/Cyrillic characters directly in the JSON (properly UTF-8 encoded)\n- Ensure your HTTP client/library is configured to handle UTF-8 encoding\n- The GitHub API will properly process and store the Cyrillic characters\n\nThe GitHub API documentation indicates standard JSON encoding practices should be followed, with UTF-8 as the default and recommended encoding for all text data.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."

**Returns:** `{ prUrl, prNumber: localPrNumber, claudeCommitHash }` (Line 1464)\n\n### 2. **Error at Line 1418 - PR Creation Failure Handling**\n\nThe error occurs in the inner error handler for PR creation (lines 1396-1420):\n\n```javascript\n} catch (prCreateError) {\n  reportError(prCreateError, {\n    context: 'pr_creation',\n    issueNumber,\n    branchName,\n    operation: 'create_pull_request',\n  });\n  // Issue #1462: Don't log verbose error block here - let the outer catch\n  // handler produce a single consolidated error message to avoid triple error output.\n  // Extract clean error message and re-throw with context.\n  const errorMsg = prCreateError.message || '';\n  let cleanError = errorMsg;\n  if (errorMsg.includes('pull request create failed:')) {\n    cleanError = errorMsg.split('pull request create failed:')[1].trim();\n  } else if (errorMsg.includes('Command failed:')) {\n    const lines = errorMsg.split('\\n');\n    cleanError = lines[lines.length - 1] || errorMsg;\n  }\n\n  if (errorMsg.includes('No commits between') || errorMsg.includes(\"Head sha can't be blank\")) {\n    throw new Error(`PR creation failed - no commits between branches: ${cleanError}`);\n  } else {\n    throw new Error(`PR creation failed: ${cleanError}`);  // LINE 1418\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 3. **How `gh pr create` is Called**\n\n**Execution Method:** Uses Node.js `child_process.exec()` with async/await pattern (Lines 1080-1121)\n\n**Key Parameters:**\n\n**For Fork PRs (Lines 1102, 1139):**\n```bash\ncd \"${tempDir}\" && gh pr create --draft \n  --title \"$(cat '${prTitleFile}')\" \n  --body-file \"${prBodyFile}\" \n  --base ${targetBranch} \n  --head ${forkUser}:${branchName} \n  --repo ${owner}/${repo}\n  [--assignee ${currentUser}]  # Optional\n```\n\n**For Non-fork PRs (Lines 1104, 1141):**\n```bash\ncd \"${tempDir}\" && gh pr create --draft \n  --title \"$(cat '${prTitleFile}')\" \n  --body-file \"${prBodyFile}\" \n  --base ${targetBranch} \n  --head ${branchName}\n  [--assignee ${currentUser}]  # Optional\n```\n\n**Special Features:**\n- **Title handling:** Uses a temporary file with `$(cat '${prTitleFile}')` to avoid shell parsing issues with special characters (quotes, apostrophes)\n- **Body handling:** Uses `--body-file` parameter with a temporary markdown file to avoid escaping issues\n- **Temp file locations:** `/tmp/pr-title-${Date.now()}.txt` and `/tmp/pr-body-${Date.now()}.md`\n- **Temp file cleanup:** Both files are deleted after command execution (Lines 1159-1172)\n\n### 4. **PR Title Construction**\n\n**PR Title Format:** `[WIP] ${issueTitle}`\n\n**Title Retrieval Process (Lines 863-874):**\n```javascript\n// Get issue title for PR title\nawait log(formatAligned('📋', 'Getting issue:', 'Title from GitHub...'), { verbose: true });\nconst issueTitleResult = await $({\n  silent: true,\n})`gh api repos/${owner}/${repo}/issues/${issueNumber} --jq .title 2>&1`;\nlet issueTitle = `Fix issue #${issueNumber}`;\nif (issueTitleResult.code === 0) {\n  issueTitle = issueTitleResult.stdout.toString().trim();\n  await log(`   Issue title: \"${issueTitle}\"`, { verbose: true });\n} else {\n  await log('   Warning: Could not get issue title, using default', { verbose: true });\n}\n```\n\n**Fallback:** If GitHub API fails, defaults to `Fix issue #${issueNumber}`\n\n### 5. **Error Handling and Retry Logic**\n\n**Multiple Layers of Error Handling:**\n\n**Layer 1: Assignee Retry (Lines 1120-1156)**\n- Primary attempt includes `--assignee` flag if user is a collaborator\n- If assignee error occurs (`could not assign user` or `not found`), retries without assignee\n- Shows warning about assignee failure and provides manual fix instructions (Lines 1264-1290)\n\n**Layer 2: PR Creation Error Handling (Lines 1396-1420)**\n- Catches any error from the `gh pr create` execution\n- Cleans up error message by removing wrapper text\n- Re-throws with context about PR creation failure\n\n**Layer 3: PR Verification (Lines 1204-1259)**\n- **CRITICAL:** Even if `gh pr create` returns a URL, verifies the PR actually exists on GitHub\n- Uses exponential backoff retry with 5 attempts (2s, 4s, 6s, 8s, 10s maximum)\n- Queries GitHub API via `gh pr view` command with JSON output parsing\n- If verification fails after all retries: throws error with both `gh pr create stderr` and `gh pr view stderr` for root cause diagnosis (Line 1258)\n\n**Example verification command (Line 1228):**\n```bash\ngh pr view ${localPrNumber} --repo ${owner}/${repo} --json number,url,state 2>&1\n```\n\n**Layer 4: Outer Error Handler (Lines 1423-1461)**\n- Catches outer try block errors\n- Provides single consolidated error message (Issue #1462)\n- Shows user-friendly troubleshooting options:\n  1. Retry without auto-PR creation\n  2. Create PR manually\n  3. Debug the issue\n\n### 6. **PR Body Construction**\n\n**Body Template (Lines 1047-1061):**\n```markdown\n## 🤖 AI-Powered Solution Draft\n\nThis pull request is being automatically generated to solve issue ${issueRef}.\n\n### 📋 Issue Reference\nFixes ${issueRef}\n\n### 🚧 Status\n**Work in Progress** - The AI assistant is currently analyzing and implementing the solution draft.\n\n### 📝 Implementation Details\n_Details will be added as the solution draft is developed..._\n\n---\n*This PR was created automatically by the AI issue solver*\n```\n\n**Issue Reference Format:**\n- **For fork PRs:** `owner/repo#issueNumber` (Line 1045)\n- **For normal PRs:** `#issueNumber`\n\n### 7. **Key Features and Safeguards**\n\n1. **File-based title/body passing:** Avoids shell injection and special character issues\n2. **API verification:** Ensures PR actually exists on GitHub (not just that the command succeeded)\n3. **Exponential backoff retries:** Handles GitHub's eventual consistency model\n4. **Assignee fallback:** Gracefully handles permission issues\n5. **Detailed error context:** Includes stderr output from both `gh pr create` and `gh pr view` commands\n6. **Branch commit validation:** Verifies commits exist before attempting PR creation (Lines 945-1034)\n7. **Global PR storage:** Stores PR info at `global.createdPR = { number: localPrNumber, url: prUrl }` (Line 1261)\n\n### 8. **How Function is Called**\n\nFrom `solve.mjs` (Lines 605-622):\n```javascript\nconst autoPrResult = await handleAutoPrCreation({\n  argv, tempDir, branchName, issueNumber, owner, repo, defaultBranch, forkedRepo,\n  isContinueMode, prNumber, log, formatAligned, $, reportError, path, fs,\n});\n\nif (autoPrResult) {\n  prUrl = autoPrResult.prUrl;\n  if (autoPrResult.prNumber) {\n    prNumber = autoPrResult.prNumber;\n  }\n  if (autoPrResult.claudeCommitHash) {\n    claudeCommitHash = autoPrResult.claudeCommitHash;\n  }\n}\n```\n\nThe function returns `null` if auto-PR creation is disabled or in continue mode with existing PR."

The user's pager (`less`) was not properly configured to handle UTF-8 encoded content. As a maintainer noted: \"GitHub CLI always outputs UTF8-encoded content,\" but the pager couldn't display it correctly.\n\n## Resolution\nThe user resolved the issue by setting an environment variable: `LESSCHARSET=utf-8`. This allowed their pager to properly interpret and display Unicode characters that the CLI was correctly outputting.\n\n## Key Takeaway\nThis demonstrates a common misconfiguration issue where the problem appears to be in the application but actually stems from downstream tool configuration. Users experiencing similar character display problems should verify their pager settings support UTF-8 encoding."

The error manifested as malformed byte sequences appearing in the error logs instead of proper UTF-8 encoding.\n\n## Root Cause\n\nThe issue wasn't directly in sgqlc's encoding logic. Instead, as the maintainer identified, the problem originated from improper use of `json.dumps()` on dictionary objects before passing them to the library. This pre-serialization introduced encoding inconsistencies before sgqlc could handle the data properly.\n\n## Resolution\n\nThe maintainer recommended using sgqlc's **Variable** system instead of embedding values directly in queries. This approach:\n\n- Keeps queries as reusable templates\n- Sends variable values separately as proper GraphQL variables\n- Ensures consistent UTF-8 encoding throughout the pipeline\n- Improves API caching and performance\n\nThe library was also updated to better handle list-type variables, resolving subsequent errors users encountered when attempting to pass array arguments through variables."

Unicode/Cyrillic Characters in gh CLI and GitHub API\n\nMy research found **no documented bug** in `gh pr create` or the GitHub GraphQL `createPullRequest` mutation specifically related to Cyrillic or non-ASCII characters in PR titles. Key findings:\n\n- **gh CLI always outputs and accepts UTF-8 encoded content** ([cli/cli Issue #3990](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/3990)). The CLI itself does not do any special character filtering on PR titles.\n- **GitHub's GraphQL API accepts UTF-8 JSON payloads**, which fully supports Cyrillic characters. The GraphQL spec and GitHub's implementation handle Unicode string values in mutation arguments without issue.\n- **No issues filed in cli/cli** match the pattern of \"Cyrillic/Russian/non-ASCII characters in PR title cause server error.\"\n- Other GraphQL implementations (Hot Chocolate, Quarkus, sgqlc) have had bugs with non-Latin characters, but these are in third-party libraries, not GitHub's server-side API. See [ChilliCream/graphql-platform Issue #885](https://github.com/ChilliCream/graphql-platform/issues/885) and [profusion/sgqlc Issue #150](https://github.com/profusion/sgqlc/issues/150).\n\n### 4. Known Causes of \"Something went wrong\" Errors\n\nThe documented causes for this generic error include:\n\n| Cause | Details |\n|-------|---------|\n| **Transient server errors** | GitHub's GraphQL API intermittently returns 500 errors that resolve on retry ([community Discussion #147768](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/147768)) |\n| **Query timeouts** | GitHub enforces a 10-second response limit; large request bodies or complex queries can timeout ([community Discussion #24631](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/24631)) |\n| **Invalid operation parameters** | e.g., selecting \"Internal\" visibility for a personal repo ([cli/cli Issue #3316](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/3316)) |\n| **Service disruptions** | GitHub platform incidents cause this error across multiple operations |\n\n### 5. Verdict: Transient Server Error vs. Reproducible Bug\n\n**This was almost certainly a transient server error caused by the March 24, 2026 GitHub service disruption**, not a bug related to Cyrillic characters in the PR title. The evidence:\n\n1. GitHub confirmed a partial outage affecting Pull Requests on exactly that date.\n2. The \"Something went wrong\" error is a generic server-side failure, not a validation or encoding error. If Cyrillic characters were invalid, the API would return a specific 422 Unprocessable Entity error with a descriptive message (e.g., \"Title contains invalid characters\").\n3. No one has filed a reproducible bug report about non-ASCII PR titles failing via the GitHub API or `gh` CLI.\n4. GitHub's API is JSON/UTF-8 based, and Cyrillic characters are perfectly valid UTF-8.\n\n### Recommendation\n\n- **Retry the command.** The service disruption has been resolved. The same `gh pr create` command with the Cyrillic title should work now.\n- If it fails again with the same error, run with `GH_DEBUG=api gh pr create ...` to capture the full HTTP request/response, which will reveal whether the issue is in the request encoding or a server-side problem.\n- If it still fails, try the PR title without Cyrillic characters as a control test to definitively rule out encoding as a factor."

resources. When `gh pr create` is called:\n    40→\n    41→1. The GitHub API accepts the PR creation request\n    42→2. GitHub returns the PR URL immediately (HTTP 201 response with Location header)\n    43→3. Different API endpoints (REST, GraphQL) may see different states during propagation\n    45→\n    46→The verification code at `src/solve.auto-pr.lib.mjs:1204-1233` queries the PR immediately after creation with **zero delay**:\n    47→\n    48→```javascript\n    49→// Line 1204-1209: Verification happens immediately after gh pr create returns\n    50→const verifyResult = await $({\n    51→  silent: true,\n    52→})`gh pr view ${localPrNumber} --repo ${owner}/${repo} --json number,url,state 2>&1`;\n    53→```\n    54→\n    55→This races against GitHub's backend propagation. In this case, the PR was created successfully (it was later merged at 07:58:46Z), but the verification check at +289ms found nothing.\n    56→\n    57→### Ironic Contrast with Existing Code\n    58→\n    59→The **same file** already implements exponential backoff retry for the compare API (lines 571-624):\n    60→\n    61→```javascript\n    62→// Line 571-584: Existing retry logic for compare API\n    63→let compareReady = false;\n    64→let compareAttempts = 0;\n    65→const maxCompareAttempts = 5;\n    66→\n    67→while (!compareReady && compareAttempts < maxCompareAttempts) {\n    68→  compareAttempts++;\n    69→  const waitTime = Math.min(2000 * compareAttempts, 10000); // 2s, 4s, 6s, 8s, 10s\n    70→  // ...\n    71→}\n    72→```\n    73→\n    74→But the PR verification step (lines 1204-1233) has **no retry logic at all**.\n    75→\n    76→### Evidence: PR Actually Existed\n    77→\n    78→The PR was later found to be:\n    79→\n    80→- **State**: closed (merged)"

This is a **race condition** caused by GitHub's eventually consistent API.\n\n## Affected Issue\n\n- **Target Repository**: Jhon-Crow/godot-topdown-MVP\n- **Target Issue**: [#1367](https://github.com/Jhon-Crow/godot-topdown-MVP/issues/1367)\n- **Created PR**: [#1368](https://github.com/Jhon-Crow/godot-topdown-MVP/pull/1368)\n- **Error**: `PR verification failed - gh pr create returned URL \"https://github.com/Jhon-Crow/godot-topdown-MVP/pull/1368\" but PR #1368 does not exist on GitHub`\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n| Timestamp (UTC)              | Event                                                            |\n| ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| 2026-03-23T04:20:15.922Z     | solve.mjs v1.35.6 enabled (no write access to target repo)               |\n| 2026-03-23T04:20:28.338Z     | Fork validated: konard/Jhon-Crow-godot-topdown-MVP               |\n| 2026-03-23T04:20:38.548Z     | |\n\n**Critical observation**: Only **289 milliseconds** elapsed between `gh pr create` returning the URL and `gh pr view` failing to find the PR.\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Primary Root Cause: GitHub API Eventual Consistency\n\nGitHub's API is **eventually consistent** for newly created resources. When `gh pr create` is called:\n\n1. The GitHub API accepts the PR creation request\n2. GitHub returns the PR URL immediately (HTTP 201 response with Location header)\n3. The PR data is written to GitHub's backend asynchronously\n4. Different API endpoints (REST, GraphQL) may see different states during propagation\n\nThe verification code at `src/solve.auto-pr.lib.mjs:1204-1233` queries the PR immediately after creation with **zero delay**:\n\n```javascript\n// Line 1204-1209: Verification happens immediately after gh pr create returns\nconst verifyResult = await $({\n  silent: true,\n})`gh pr view ${localPrNumber} --repo ${owner}/${repo} --json number,url,state 2>&1`;\n```\n\nThis races against GitHub's backend propagation. In this case, the PR was created successfully (it was later merged at 07:58:46Z), but the verification check at +289ms found nothing.\n\n### Ironic Contrast with Existing Code\n\nThe **same file** already implements exponential backoff retry for the compare API (lines 571-624):\n\n```javascript\n// Line 571-584: Existing retry logic for compare API\nlet compareReady = false;\nlet compareAttempts = 0;\nconst maxCompareAttempts = 5;\n\nwhile (!compareReady && compareAttempts < maxCompareAttempts) {\n  compareAttempts++;\n  const waitTime = Math.min(2000 * compareAttempts, 10000); // 2s, 4s, 6s, 8s, 10s\n  // ...\n}\n```\n\nBut the PR verification step (lines 1204-1233) has **no retry logic at all**.\n\n### Evidence: PR Actually Existed\n\nThe PR was later found to be:\n\n- **State**: closed (merged)",

         if (currentUser && canAssign) {\n            command += ` --assignee ${currentUser}`;\n          }\n\n          if (argv.verbose) {\n            await log(`   Command: ${command}`, { verbose: true });\n          }\n\n          let output;\n          let prCreateStderr = '';\n          let assigneeFailed = false;\n\n          // Try to create PR with assignee first (if specified)\n          try {\n            const result = await execAsync(command, { encoding: 'utf8', cwd: tempDir, env: process.env });\n            output = result.stdout;\n            prCreateStderr = result.stderr || '';\n          } catch (firstError) {\n            // Check if the error is specifically about assignee validation\n            const errorMsg = firstError.message || '';\n            if ((errorMsg.includes('could not assign user') || errorMsg.includes('not found')) && currentUser && canAssign) {\n              // Assignee validation failed - retry without assignee\n              assigneeFailed = true;\n              await log('');\n              await log(formatAligned('⚠️', 'Warning:', `User assignment failed for '${currentUser}'`), {\n                level: 'warning',\n              });\n              await log('     Retrying PR creation without assignee...');\n\n              // Rebuild command without --assignee flag\n              if (argv.fork && forkedRepo) {\n                const forkUser = forkedRepo.split('/')[0];\n                command = `cd \"${tempDir}\" && gh pr create --draft --title \"$(cat '${prTitleFile}')\" --body-file \"${prBodyFile}\" --base ${targetBranch} --head ${forkUser}:${branchName} --repo ${owner}/${repo}`;\n              } else {\n                command = `cd \"${tempDir}\" && gh pr create --draft --title \"$(cat '${prTitleFile}')\" --body-file \"${prBodyFile}\" --base ${targetBranch} --head ${branchName}`;\n              }\n\n              if (argv.verbose) {\n                await log(`   Retry command (without assignee): ${command}`, { verbose: true });\n              }\n\n              // Retry without assignee - if this fails, let the error propagate to outer catch\n              const retryResult = await execAsync(command, { encoding: 'utf8', cwd: tempDir, env: process.env });\n              output = retryResult.stdout;\n              prCreateStderr = retryResult.stderr || '';\n            } else {\n              // Not an assignee error, re-throw the original error\n              throw firstError;\n            }\n          }\n\n          // Clean up temp files\n          await fs.unlink(prBodyFile).catch(unlinkError => {\n            reportError(unlinkError, {\n              context: 'pr_body_file_cleanup',\n              prBodyFile,\n              operation: 'delete_temp_file',\n            });\n          });\n          await fs.unlink(prTitleFile).catch(unlinkError => {\n            reportError(unlinkError, {\n              context: 'pr_title_file_cleanup',\n              prTitleFile,\n              operation: 'delete_temp_file',\n            });\n          });\n\n          // Log gh pr create output for debugging (Issue #1462)\n          if (argv.verbose) {\n            await log(`   gh pr create stdout: ${(output || '').trim() || '(empty)'}`, { verbose: true });\n            if (prCreateStderr) {\n              await log(`   gh pr create stderr: ${prCreateStderr.trim()}`, { verbose: true });\n            }\n          }\n\n          // Extract PR URL from output - gh pr create outputs the URL to stdout\n          prUrl = output.trim();\n\n          if (!prUrl) {\n            await log('⚠️ Warning: PR created but no URL returned', { level: 'warning' });\n            await log(`   Output: ${output}`, { verbose: true The error occurred during a confirmed GitHub service disruption on March 24, 2026. The PR title contained Cyrillic characters (\"update враг снайпер\"), which was initially suspected as a cause but was ruled out — the failure was a transient server-side error with no retry mechanism in place.\n\n## Affected Issue\n\n- **Target Repository**: Jhon-Crow/godot-topdown-MVP\n- **Target Issue**: [#1336](https://github.com/Jhon-Crow/godot-topdown-MVP/issues/1336) — \"update враг снайпер\"\n- **Error**: `GraphQL: Something went wrong while executing your query on 2026-03-24T20:09:47Z. Please include C494:160A:1899070D:156DE0AF:69C2EF89 when reporting this issue.`\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n| Timestamp (UTC)              | Event                                                            |\n| ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| 2026-03-24T20:09:14.090Z     | solve.mjs v1.35.9 started                                        |\n| 2026-03-24T20:09:19.720Z     | System checks passed (disk, memory)                              |\n| 2026-03-24T20:09:20.501Z     | Fork mode enabled (no write access to target repo)               |\n| 2026-03-24T20:09:26.907Z     | Fork validated: konard/Jhon-Crow-godot-topdown-MVP               |\n| 2026-03-24T20:09:37.398Z     | Repository cloned to /tmp/gh-issue-solver-1774382965260          |\n| 2026-03-24T20:09:39.082Z     | Branch created: issue-1336-9d97d520d1f8                          |\n| 2026-03-24T20:09:39.155Z     | .gitkeep committed: 4e6145a5                                     |\n| 2026-03-24T20:09:40.083Z     | Branch pushed to remote (exit code 0)                            |\n| 2026-03-24T20:09:42.587Z     | Compare API confirms: 1 commit ahead of main                     |\n| 2026-03-24T20:09:43.526Z     | Issue title fetched: \"update враг снайпер\"                       |\n| 2026-03-24T20:09:44.432Z     | `gh pr create --draft` command executed                          |\n| **2026-03-24T20:09:47.583Z** | **FATAL ERROR: GraphQL server-side error (3.1s after command)**  |\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\n### Primary Root Cause: Transient GitHub API Server Error\n\nThe error `\"Something went wrong while executing your query\"` is a **generic GitHub server-side error** (HTTP 500-class). The hex code `C494:160A:1899070D:156DE0AF:69C2EF89` is an internal GitHub correlation ID for their engineering team to locate the specific failure.\n\n**GitHub confirmed a service disruption on March 24, 2026** titled \"Disruption with some GitHub services\" (incident code: `kp06czybl7dw`) affecting:\n- Pull Requests — ~1,941 seconds of downtime\n- Issues — ~1,940 seconds of downtime\n- Git Operations — ~5,006 seconds of downtime\n\nThe error timestamp `2026-03-24T20:09:47Z` falls within this disruption window.\n\n### Red Herring: Cyrillic Characters\n\nThe PR title `[WIP] update враг снайпер` contains Cyrillic characters. Initial investigation considered whether non-ASCII characters in the title caused the GraphQL error. This was **ruled out** because:\n\n1. GitHub's GraphQL API accepts UTF-8 JSON payloads, which fully supports Cyrillic\n2. The code already uses file-based title passing (`$(cat '/tmp/pr-title-*.txt')`) to avoid shell encoding issues\n3. No documented bugs exist in `gh` CLI or GitHub API related to non-ASCII PR titles\n4. If Cyrillic were invalid, GitHub would return a 422 Unprocessable Entity with a descriptive validation error, not a generic 500 \"something went wrong\"\n5. The error coincided with a confirmed GitHub service disruption\n\n### Secondary Root Cause: Missing Retry Logic for PR Creation\n\nWhile the codebase already has retry logic with exponential backoff for:\n- **Compare API** (lines 571-624): 5 retries with 2s, 4s, 6s, 8s, 10s backoff\n- **PR verification** (lines 1206-1259): 5 retries with same backoff pattern\n\nThe `gh pr create` command itself (line 1121) has **no retry logic for transient server errors**. It only retries for assignee validation errors (lines 1124-1156). When a transient GitHub API error occurs, the tool immediately fails without any retry attempt.\n\n## Evidence\n\n### Sources Consulted\n- [cli/cli Issue #7735](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/7735) — Same generic GraphQL error with `gh pr merge --auto`\n- [cli/cli Issue #3316](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/3316) — Same error with `gh repo create`\n- [cli/cli Issue #4037](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/4037) — Same error pattern in other operations\n- [GitHub Status Page](https://www.githubstatus.com) — Confirmed disruption on 2026-03-24\n\n### Key Code Locations\n- `src/solve.auto-pr.lib.mjs:1121` — `execAsync(command, ...)` call with no retry\n- `src/solve.auto-pr.lib.mjs:1396-1420` — Error handler that immediately throws\n- `src/solve.auto-pr.lib.mjs:571-624` — Existing retry pattern (compare API)\n- `src/solve.auto-pr.lib.mjs:1206-1259` — Existing retry pattern (PR verification)\n\n## Proposed Solution\n\nAdd retry logic with exponential backoff for the `gh pr create` command when transient server errors are detected. The retry should:\n\n1. **Detect transient errors**: Match patterns like \"Something went wrong\", HTTP 500, \"502 Bad Gateway\", \"503 Service Unavailable\", and network errors\n2. **Use existing backoff pattern**: 5 retries with `Math.min(2000 * attempt, 10000)` delays (2s, 4s, 6s, 8s, 10s)\n3. **Only retry transient errors**: Non-transient errors (validation, auth, \"No commits between\") should still fail immediately\n4. **Log retry attempts**: Using existing verbose logging pattern\n\n### Implementation Reference\n\nThe retry logic follows the same pattern already established at lines 571-624 and 1206-1259 of `solve.auto-pr.lib.mjs`.\n"

errors during PR creation.\n          // Uses the same exponential backoff pattern as the compare API (lines 571-624)\n          // and PR verification (lines 1206-1259) retries.\n          const maxPrCreateAttempts = 3;\n          let prCreateAttempt = 0;\n\n          while (prCreateAttempt < maxPrCreateAttempts) {\n            prCreateAttempt++;\n\n            try {\n              // Try to }\n\n                  // Retry without assignee - if this fails, let the error propagate\n                  const retryResult = await execAsync(command, { encoding: 'utf8', cwd: tempDir, env: process.env });\n                  output = retryResult.stdout;\n                  prCreateStderr = retryResult.stderr || '';\n                } else {\n                  // Not an assignee error, validation/auth errors\n              if (isTransientGitHubError(attemptErrorMsg) && prCreateAttempt < maxPrCreateAttempts) {\n                const waitTime = Math.min(5000 * prCreateAttempt, 15000); // 5s, 10s, 15s\n                await log('');\n                await log(formatAligned('⚠️', 'Warning:', `Transient GitHub API error during PR creation (attempt
